title: elaphoglossum pseudoerinaceum
intro: Elaphoglossum pseudoerinaceum, also known as the false hedgehog fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Elaphoglossum pseudoerinaceum include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, and are often used to treat skin conditions, respiratory issues, and other health concerns.
